exclurad.F.name: polarization_mode_comment_1
exclurad.F.description: Commented-out ipolmax=1 alternative (cosmetic; the active data ipolmax/2/ is common to both).

exclurad.F.name: polarization_mode_comment_2
exclurad.F.description: Explanatory comments for ipolmax (1 = unpolarized only, saves 50% time; 2 = both, required for beam-spin asymmetry).

eta:
*ipolmax = 1 only unpolarized case
*ipolmax = 2 polarized case
*ipolmax for only unpol will save 50% of time

exclurad.F.name: output_format_radasm_comment
exclurad.F.description: Commented-out CSV format for radasm.dat (unit 27). Active format is fixed-width 5f6.2,5F8.3 in both channels.

exclurad.F.name: output_format_radsigpl
exclurad.F.description: radsigpl.dat (unit 41) write format: CSV F12.6 in eta, fixed-width 5f6.2,2g11.3 in pion. Downstream parsers must match (see outputs.py).

exclurad.F.name: output_format_radsigmi_comment
exclurad.F.description: Commented-out CSV format for radsigmi.dat (unit 42). Active format is fixed-width in both channels.

exclurad.F.name: output_format_radtot
exclurad.F.description: radtot.dat (unit 28) columns AND format. Eta: 10 columns CSV F16.10 (e1,w,q2,cos,phi,delta,sigma_born,as,asb,dd). Pion: 7 columns fixed-width 5f6.2,5F8.3 (e1,w,q2,cos,phi,ddcr,dd).

exclurad.F.name: meson_mass_amhad
exclurad.F.description: Detected/undetected hadron mass arrays amhad/amunm in the main kinematics [GeV]. SITE 1 of 4 where the meson mass is hardcoded — all four must agree.

exclurad.F.name: integration_tolerance
exclurad.F.description: Photon phase-space integration tolerance 'ot'. Eta build tightened it to 1d-3 (with a commented 1d-4 alternative); pion uses 1d-2.

exclurad.F.name: meson_mass_born_1
exclurad.F.description: XMPIP/XMPI0 masses in Born structure-function code [GeV]. SITE 2 of 4 for the meson mass.

exclurad.F.name: meson_mass_born_2
exclurad.F.description: XMPIP/XMPI0/XMP masses (higher precision) in a second Born routine. SITE 3 of 4 for the meson mass.

exclurad.F.name: meson_mass_born_3
exclurad.F.description: XMPIP/XMPI0 masses in a third routine. SITE 4 of 4 for the meson mass.

exclurad.F.name: table_header_format
exclurad.F.description: Fortran FORMAT for reading the 'W = ... Q2 = ...' block headers of the lookup table. Must match the table file generation: EtaMAID-2023 vs MAID07 header layouts differ.

mpintp.inc.name: nvar_grid_dims
mpintp.inc.description: NVAR1 x NVAR2 = (Q2, W) grid dimensions of the lookup table; MUST match the .tbl file. Victor's comments enumerate the historically accepted values per table generation.

eta:
c!!!Victor!!!   PARAMETER( NVAR1  = 101 , NVAR2 = 93 ! Q2,W ! Q2, W maid07
c      PARAMETER( NVAR1  = 101 , NVAR2 = 53 )	! Q2,W ! Q2, W EtaMAID2000

c!!!Victor!!! Parameters for EtaMAID2000:
c      PARAMETER( NVAR1  = 51 )	! Q2 = 0 - 5 GeV² with step 0.1 GeV²
c      PARAMETER( NVAR1  = 61 )	! Q2 = 0 - 6 GeV² with step 0.1 GeV²
c      PARAMETER( NVAR1  = 121)	! Q2 = 0 - 6 GeV² with step 0.05 GeV²

c      PARAMETER( NVAR2 = 53 )	! W = 1.4856 - 2 GeV with step 10 MeV
c      PARAMETER( NVAR2 = 104 )	! W = 1.4856 - 2 GeV with step 5 MeV

c!!!Victor!!! Parameters for EtaMAID2023:
      PARAMETER( NVAR1 = 101 )	! Q2 = 0 - 5 GeV² with step 0.05 GeV²
c-(1th: W=1485.6 MeV) + 51(1500-2000 MeV, dW=10MeV) + 40(2100-6000 MeV, dW=100 MeV) =92
      PARAMETER( NVAR2 = 92 )	! W = 1.4856 - 6 GeV with step 100 MeV
pion: PARAMETER( NVAR1  = 101 , NVAR2 = 93 )	! Q2,W ! Q2, W maid 07
